model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Duke holds a commanding 10-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over UConn (+39.17 vs +29.0), driven by the nation's best defense (AdjD 88.7) that should significantly limit UConn's 26th-ranked offense, while Duke's 4th-ranked offense (AdjO 127.9) has the firepower to challenge even UConn's excellent 11th-ranked defense. The Blue Devils have adapted well to the loss of starting point guard Caleb Foster, with Cayden Boozer stepping into an expanded role and Cooper Flagg performing at a generational level, while UConn's 72-52 loss to St. John's in the Big East Tournament championship exposed vulnerabilities that Duke's elite two-way profile is built to exploit. UConn's championship pedigree under Dan Hurley and their own elite defense give them a credible path to an upset, but the quality gap across every dimension—particularly Duke's historically dominant defense—makes the Blue Devils the clear favorite to advance to the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: East Regional Final: Duke vs UConn
reasoningStep.summary: Duke's historically dominant two-way profile, anchored by the nation's #1 defense and a top-4 offense, creates a roughly 10-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over a strong but clearly inferior UConn team. While UConn's championship DNA and elite defense make them a worthy opponent, the quality gap is too wide for a 2-seed to overcome against the tournament's top overall team.
evidence: Duke KenPom #1 (AdjEM +39.17, AdjO 127.9 4th, AdjD 88.7 best nationally) vs UConn KenPom #9 (AdjEM +29.0, AdjO 123.0 26th, AdjD 94.0 11th)
Duke Torvik #1 (AdjOE 128.0 4th, AdjDE 90.4 1st) vs UConn Torvik #9 (AdjOE 124.1 20th, AdjDE 94.6 11th)
Duke 31-2 record; UConn 29-4 record with a 72-52 loss to St. John's in Big East Tournament championship
Duke missing Caleb Foster (broken foot) and Patrick Ngongba but have adapted; UConn missing Jaylin Stewart (knee, bench role player)
Cooper Flagg healthy and performing at generational level; UConn led by Tarris Reed Jr., Alex Karaban, Solo Ball
~10-point efficiency margin gap is enormous for a 1-vs-2 seed Elite Eight matchup

Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me synthesize all the data for this Elite 8 matchup.

**Key Data Summary:**

**Duke (1-seed):**
- KenPom: #1, AdjEM +39.17, AdjO 127.9 (4th), AdjD 88.7 (best in nation)
- Torvik: #1, AdjOE 128.0 (4th), AdjDE 90.4 (1st)
- Record: 31-2
- Injuries: Caleb Foster OUT (broken foot), Patrick Ngongba OUT; Cooper Flagg healthy and dominant (dropped 30 in Sweet 16 last year vs Arizona)
- Cameron Boozer, Cooper Flagg, Cayden Boozer leading the way

**UConn (2-seed):**
- KenPom: #9, AdjEM +29.0, AdjO 123.0 (26th), AdjD 94.0 (11th)
- Torvik: #9, AdjOE 124.1 (20th), AdjDE 94.6 (11th)
- Record: 29-4
- Key players: Tarris Reed Jr., Alex Karaban, Solo Ball, Silas Demary Jr.
- Injuries: Jaylin Stewart missed final 6 games with knee injury (bench role player)
- Dan Hurley chasing potential 3rd title in 4 years
- Lost Big East Tournament championship to St. John's 72-52

**Efficiency Gap:** Duke's +39.17 vs UConn's +29.0 = roughly 10-point gap per 100 possessions. This is a massive difference even for a 1-vs-2 seed matchup.

**Key Matchup Dynamics:**
- Duke's #1 defense (AdjD 88.7) vs UConn's solid offense (AdjO 123.0, 26th) — Duke's defense should significantly limit UConn
- Duke's elite offense (AdjO 127.9, 4th) vs UConn's elite defense (AdjD 94.0, 11th) — this is the competitive matchup, but Duke's offense is still likely to produce
- Duke has a roughly 5-point defensive advantage AND a roughly 5-point offensive advantage
- Duke missing Foster hurts but they've adapted; UConn missing Stewart is less impactful

The Blue Devils have adapted well to the loss of starting point guard Caleb Foster, with Cayden Boozer stepping into an expanded role and Cooper Flagg performing at a generational level, while UConn's loss to St. John's 72-52 in the Big East Tournament championship exposed vulnerabilities that Duke's elite two-way profile is built to exploit.
